title: Kaji Kaworu and the Cat
wikitext:
[]: Kaji, Kaworu and the Cat Post by Silent Lightning » Sun Oct 05, 2008 4:48 pm This was a connection I made after reading one of Shin-Seiki's painstakingly-researched ANF threads; I hope my train of thought isn't too hard to follow. There've been speculations that Ritsuko's "cat", which we don't ever seem to see in person (in cat?), is in fact a reference to Kaji, given the timing of their respective deaths and Ritsuko's sentiments towards each. Ritsuko's otherwise quite random mention of her "cat" during her interrogation by Gendo, as well as Kaji's gift to Ritsuko at the wedding, also seem to fit into this. That's not to say there haven't been several counter-arguments against this, most famously Reichu-sama's Reichu wrote: Sometimes, a cat is just a cat.

[]: Post by AyrYntake » Sun Oct 05, 2008 4:58 pm Wow, nicely researched. Well done.

[]: Post by Zerogoki » Sun Oct 05, 2008 5:47 pm Some interesting thoughts there. I'll be honest and say that I'm skeptical about the link between the death of the 'cat' and Kaworu arriving, although I nonetheless I like that someone is having a go at it, as the 'cat' issue has been bugging me for years. I really like this bit: What I'm trying to propose here is that Ritsuko's "cat" is in fact an abstract notion, a representation of her hope for happiness

[]: Re: Kaji, Kaworu and the Cat Post by NemZ » Sun Oct 05, 2008 6:10 pm I'm not buying it. I don't claim to know what the cat scene means (if anything more than exactly what it appears to be) but you've got some problems. Silent Lightning wrote: But Ritsuko knew about Kaworu before anyone else at Nerv did, not to mention having a knowledge of his true purpose, noting the "final messenger" line when Misato vists the isolated Ritsuko.

[]: Re: Kaji, Kaworu and the Cat Post by Reichu » Sun Oct 05, 2008 7:33 pm Silent Lightning wrote: There've been speculations that Ritsuko's "cat", which we don't ever seem to see in person (in cat?)

[]: Post by V » Sun Oct 05, 2008 7:57 pm ......the hint of Kaworu's appearance.....is when Ritsuko leans in and whispers to Maya in episode 22 to start prepping a replacement pilot for Asuka.....

[]: Post by AyrYntake » Mon Oct 06, 2008 2:10 am Here's something else to think about: why does Ritsuko so hurriedly put Kaji's cat-souvenir into her bag before Misato comes along and notices?

[]: Post by Mr. Tines » Mon Oct 06, 2008 2:12 am She doesn't want to get into a cat-fight with Misato over Kaji.

[]: Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon Oct 06, 2008 1:09 pm Silent Lightning wrote: Ritsuko appearing naked before Seele also poses a strange problem, namely, well, WHY? I'm sure there are reasons beyond the Committee just being a bunch of pervs, or to humiliate her and break down her bond with Gendo.

[]: Post by Mr. Tines » Mon Oct 06, 2008 1:15 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: An archived ANF thread came to the conclusion that Seele's goons raped Ritsuko.

[]: Post by Silent Lightning » Mon Oct 06, 2008 4:55 pm Well, it's nice to hear that my idea hasn't been TOTALLY trashed...

[]: Post by Reichu » Mon Oct 06, 2008 9:00 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: This I agree with. Reichu pointed out parallels between the two (as well as Dr. Katsuragi and Misato) in this ancient ANF thread. KKKK theory I think it was called.

[]: Post by Silent Lightning » Tue Oct 07, 2008 4:36 am Wow thanks there's an absolute wealth of information in here. Has an actual explanation for the Kaji/Kaworu similarity been put forward yet?

[]: Post by Zaiku » Tue Oct 07, 2008 6:28 am Its an interesting theory. Also, I thought that it would be useful to note, even though it rather coincides with the tiers of canonicity, in the Manga, Kaworu actually KILLS a cat when he first meets Shinji. I don't remember why, I think it was to put it out of its misery, like it was in pain or something.

[]: Post by Mr. Tines » Tue Oct 07, 2008 11:33 am Kaworu killed the orphan kitten to spare it a slow death in the ruins. Or because Shinji fapped that morning.

[]: Post by AuraTwilight » Tue Oct 07, 2008 1:09 pm And because he's an Evil Albino. Who's Jesus in Purgatory.

[]: Post by Reichu » Tue Oct 07, 2008 2:14 pm Zaiku wrote: Its an interesting theory. Also, I thought that it would be useful to note, even though it rather coincides with the tiers of canonicity, in the Manga, Kaworu actually KILLS a cat when he first meets Shinji.

[]: Post by VoidEater » Tue Oct 07, 2008 7:46 pm Tangential, but inescapble: I don't understand the resistance to Kaworu being open to a romantic relationship with Shiji. While Kaworu does not seem focused on a sexual relationship, clearly the subject of sexuality as a means for people to emotionally connect with each other is being treated here, and clearly Kaworu has no inhibitions in approaching Shinji with that possibility. The protests against this bonding seem to contradict something obvious in the plot. That Shinji doesn't "connect" with the invitation is an interesting dynamic, it seems he isn't clear about what Kaworu is suggesting. Kaworu, having a SoL soul, isn't likely concerned about gender from a philosoophical point of view, and might be seen as pansexual. Kaworu's counterpart, Rei, having the same bases, might be seen as the asexual counterpoint to Kaworu - making her rejection of Gendo more poignant. This may not speak to Shinji's sexuality, although in terms of character development we might say this: Shinji not acting on Kaworu's invitation allows Anno to show that his discomfort with female intimacy is not because he is attracted to his own gender.

[]: Post by Sailor Star Dust » Tue Oct 07, 2008 7:54 pm Shinji's attitude around Kaworu does have some sexual confusion, but before and after Kaworu's arrival the series makes it clear where his affections lie. And also there's the idea which I agree with that Shinji killing Kaworu is his way of giving up the ideal (Instrumentality) for the real (reality).

[]: Post by Lucretius » Tue Oct 07, 2008 8:00 pm VoidEater wrote: Shinji not acting on Kaworu's invitation allows Anno to show that his discomfort with female intimacy is not because he is attracted to his own gender.

There've been speculations that Ritsuko's "cat", which we don't ever seem to see in person (in cat?), is in fact a reference to Kaji, given the timing of their respective deaths and Ritsuko's sentiments towards each.
